claim_boundary: Professional Phase 3 prepares internal thresholds and professional-review questions only. It is not legal advice, financial advice, tax advice, valuation advice, binding contract language, or an offer.
core_rule: If procurement, paid pilot, contract terms, budget, or money movement becomes real, engage CPA/legal support before proceeding alone.
cpa_questions: How should paid pilot revenue be recognized?
What bookkeeping system and chart of accounts should be used?
How should owner contributions, reimbursements, software/tooling costs, cloud/API costs, and professional fees be tracked?
What records should be retained for audit/tax readiness?
How should large first payments, retainers, deposits, or milestone payments be handled?
What tax issues arise for out-of-state or international clients?
Should payments be separated by pilot, license, consulting, reimbursement, or product revenue?
What reserves should be held for taxes and professional fees?
How should contractor/vendor costs be documented if used later?
What financial controls are needed before accepting client funds?
forbidden_self_handling: do not accept major payment without CPA/legal review
do not grant exclusivity without lawyer review
do not transfer code or protected methods without lawyer review
do not promise compliance, production monitoring, truth validation, or guaranteed outcomes
do not provide legal, financial, medical, or regulated advice to clients
lawyer_questions: What claims can SpecShift safely make publicly, privately, and in buyer scoping?
What should the standard NDA/MSA/SOW/pilot agreement include?
How should liability, warranty disclaimers, indemnity, and limitation-of-liability clauses be handled?
How should prohibited-use language be written and enforced?
How should protected methods, source code, trade secrets, and non-use be protected?
What client data retention, deletion, confidentiality, and security terms are needed?
What IP ownership language is needed for outputs, methods, reports, and derivative work?
What can be shared in a first-send packet versus after NDA?
What should be reviewed before any paid pilot, procurement step, or contract discussion?
What insurance, DPA, security, or compliance posture might buyers request?

exceptions: public demonstration using synthetic or non-client data
short scoping call with no protected disclosure
text-only overview that does not transfer protected methods or artifacts
no_free_pilot_posture.reason: Free pilots risk uncompensated value transfer, method leakage, ambiguous obligations, and weak buyer commitment.
no_free_pilot_posture.safe_language: I can discuss a bounded paid pilot with written scope, success criteria, data boundaries, and human-review controls.

paid_pilot_handling.boundary: Prices are internal planning anchors only, not quotes, offers, legal terms, valuation, or tax advice.
minimum_before_accepting: systems in scope and out of scope
buyer retains labels and final adjudication

purpose: Prepare CPA/lawyer trigger thresholds, paid-pilot handling, no-free-pilot posture, and professional-review questions for SpecShift.

trigger_events.description: A buyer expresses interest in paying for a pilot, evaluation, proof of concept, or trial.
trigger_events.required_action: Pause before accepting money or terms; prepare scope and ask lawyer/CPA what structure is appropriate.

trigger_events.description: A buyer asks for vendor onboarding, procurement paperwork, insurance, tax forms, security review, or contract review.
A buyer sends MSA, NDA, SOW, DPA, security terms, IP terms, indemnity, warranties, or limitation-of-liability language.
trigger_events.required_action: Do not sign alone; route to lawyer.

trigger_events.description: A buyer asks for price, budget range, payment terms, invoice handling, or pilot fee.
trigger_events.required_action: Use non-binding range language only until CPA/lawyer review.

trigger_events.description: Any payment, deposit, reimbursement, grant, award, wire, ACH, platform payment, or client-paid expense becomes likely.
trigger_events.required_action: CPA review before receiving or moving funds.

trigger_events.description: A buyer asks for source code, protected methods, implementation details, training rights, model internals, or exclusive access.
trigger_events.required_action: Decline or defer until lawyer review.
